title: huperzia selago subsp. lucidula
intro: Huperzia selago subsp. lucidula, also known as the fir club moss, is a species of club moss in the family Huperziaceae. It is a type of evergreen plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Asia and Europe, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to inhibit acetylcholinesterase and increase dopamine levels in the brain. The health benefits of Huperzia selago subsp. lucidula include improved memory and cognitive function, as well as potential neuroprotective effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in capsule or powder form to support brain health and cognitive function.
